threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus is on time. Locals know it’s just a suggestion and plan their lives around those delays.
Just spotted a fellow on the 36 singing opera like he was back in the Royal Albert Hall. Only in Peckham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was really in Neukölln when I spotted a kid selling homemade cookies outside the U-Bahn station, and nobody batted an eye.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see spontaneous techno parties in abandoned factories. It’s a unique blend of creativity and chaos you won’t find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You’ll spot a local if they instinctively know to avoid Civic Center BART at rush hour, even before all the smoke and chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy juggling at the Civic Center stop while a bus driver was handing out snacks. Only in San Francisco do transit delays turn into street performances.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every plaza is a photo op, but locals see them as echo chambers for street musicians. There’s a rhythm to it, different every day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their bewildered look at the metro map, still trying to wrap their head around the L3 and L5 connections.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know someone’s new when they ask how to get to Shoreditch and don’t realize it’s a twenty-minute walk, not a Tube ride.
Caught a glimpse of a fox casually trotting alongside the 133 last night. Only in Brixton do the street animals have that level of chill.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid rollerblading past a kebab shop, while an older couple shared a bottle of wine on the curb. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery, where murals change monthly. It's a feast for your eyes and stomach.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local rolls their eyes at tourists fumbling with the BART map—while they navigate the delays and hot brakes like it's a rite of passage.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the bus stop crowd with an ukulele while a rat scuttled past. Only in the Tenderloin does the Muni stop feel like a mini show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all Gràcia bars close at 2 AM, but many stay open until dawn—just depends which ones. The locals know the good spots.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their excitement at ordering 'pan con tomate' but still pronouncing it wrong, like 'tomato bread' in English.
